TextENGNew: After so many detours, after passing through the wishes and schemes of so many people... at last I was able to take hold of this piece.
changeReason: Old translates '思惑' as 'thoughts' (neutral); new uses 'wishes and schemes,' which captures the JPN connotation of strategic intentions and ulterior motives.

TextENGNew: And yet this one is entirely different from the others — its shape, its color... even the weight it carries feels nothing the same.
...Why, you ask?
Because... this piece does not contain only the fate of Rika Furude.
Until now, we had thought only of how Rika could find happiness — nothing else.
For that purpose, we joined our strength and tried again and again to scale that wall.
But every one of those efforts was repelled by Takano's will — her fierce conviction, her relentless determination.
Then — does that mean gathering our feelings and our strength alone is not enough to defeat Takano?
Do we need more powerful pieces still?
Or must we wish for a miracle that lies beyond the bounds of common sense?
...No. That is not it.
What we needed was a shift in perspective.
Takano does stand in our way with enormous power and iron will — that much is true.
But... just where does that power come from?
And what is it that sustains that will?
Unraveling those questions and tracing the lifeline that extended long and steady behind her... there was something there — another presence we had never noticed.
changeReason: Old translates '別の存在' as 'another patron' (implying a specific supporter/sponsor role), while new uses 'another presence,' which is more faithful to the deliberately vague JPN.

TextENGNew: Then — should we simply defeat that?
...No. Our reach is far too short for that.
And against an opponent more powerful even than Takano, there is no guarantee that we — who cannot even defeat her — could prevail.
changeReason: New includes '彼女さえ倒せない私たち' (we who cannot even defeat her) -- an important qualifier present in the JPN that the old translation omitted entirely.

TextENGNew: If that is so... there is only one method.
Sever that lifeline.
And the means to do so was found in an unexpected place.
